Literature Digest

Jun 01 – Jun 15, 2026  ·  Chuong Lab, CU Boulder
1005 scanned
12 preprints
20/20 journals
keyword + AI

AI Summary

This digest features two landmark transposable element papers of direct relevance to the lab. Ho, Douse & Marazzi (Cell) reframe TEs as locus-specific regulatory drivers, shifting from family-level to individual-copy analysis enabled by long-read sequencing, directly validating the lab paradigm. Zhuo et al. (Genome Research) leverage human pangenome resources to characterize cytosine methylation at polymorphic TE insertions, finding locus-specific methylation with cis-effects on gene expression. Dayama et al. show TE-derived RNAs accumulate in aging brains and are dysregulated in Huntington and Parkinson disease. A Genome Biology study finds piRNAs preferentially associate with active enhancers near TE insertions in germ cells.

The cGAS-STING pathway dominates the immunity section. Zhu et al. (Immunity) dissect how TREX1 modular dsDNA recognition degrades cytosolic TE-derived DNA before it engages cGAS. Gao et al. (Cell) show bacterial CBASS systems use cGAMP to activate membrane-shearing effectors, illuminating the deep evolutionary origin of cGAS-STING signaling. On the cancer front, Tishina et al. demonstrate oncogenic KRAS induces type I IFN signaling that primes pancreatic cancer cells for necroptosis. The prominence of APOL1 in a new Nature Genetics proteomics study is a reminder the lab MER41-APOL1 regulatory axis remains biologically significant.

Curated Highlights 21 papers · 5 topics

TE Biology 6
Transposable element DNA and RNA: Drivers of gene expression, evolution, and disease.
Jun 11, 2026 · Ho Jessica Sook Yuin, Douse Christopher H, Marazzi Ivan · Cell
A Cell review reframes TEs as locus-specific regulatory drivers of gene expression, chromatin organization, and disease across mammals. → article
Characterizing cytosine methylation of polymorphic transposable element insertions using the human pangenome resources.
Jun 01, 2026 · Zhuo Xiaoyu, Wang Ting et al. · Genome Research
Long-read pangenome sequencing reveals locus-specific cytosine methylation at polymorphic TE insertions with cis-regulatory effects in human LCLs. → article
Conserved enhancer association of piRNAs and the implication in germ cell fate surveillance
Jun 11, 2026 · Unknown · Genome Biology
Pre-pachytene piRNAs are enriched at active enhancers proximal to TE insertions in germ cells, linking the piRNA pathway to enhancer-proximal gene regulation. → article
Transposable element small RNAs and large RNAs in aging brains and implications in Huntington's and Parkinson's disease.
Jun 01, 2026 · Dayama Gargi, Lau Nelson C et al. · Genome Research
TE-derived small and large RNAs accumulate in aging human and mouse brains and are further dysregulated in Huntington and Parkinson disease. → article
Ancient but dynamic: structural expansion, massive gene duplication, and transposable element colonization in a supergene controlling ant social organization.
Jun 04, 2026 · Boulain Hélène, Chapuisat Michel et al. · Molecular Biology and Evolution
The 30-million-year-old Formica ant social supergene expanded via massive gene duplication and transposable element colonization. → article
IS110 transposon utilizes two mechanistically distinct RNA-guided transposition pathways
Jun 01, 2026 · Xuanlong Sun, Chen Yang, Zhaohui Cai, Li Liu, Xinmin Yue, Shuquan Rao, Huiqiang Lou, Chaoyou Xue · Molecular Cell
IS110 transposons use two mechanistically distinct RNA-guided pathways, copy-out and direct strand transfer, for site-specific transposition. → article
Immunity & Inflammation 10
Modular double-stranded DNA recognition defines specificity of the exonuclease TREX1 in cGAS-STING control.
Jun 09, 2026 · Zhu Jiali, Zhou Wen et al. · Immunity
TREX1 uses a modular dsDNA recognition domain absent in other DEDDh exonucleases to selectively degrade cytosolic DNA and suppress cGAS-STING activation. → article
2'3'-cGAMP-induced membrane shearing promotes broad antiphage immunity.
Jun 11, 2026 · Gao Yina, Gao Pu et al. · Cell
Bacterial cGAMP triggers membrane-shearing effector proteins in CBASS antiphage systems, revealing a cytolytic mechanism for cyclic nucleotide immunity. → article
Targeting the cGAS-STING pathway mitigates Huntington disease pathogenesis in a knock-in mouse model.
Jun 16, 2026 · Kesharwani Anuradha, Subramaniam Srinivasa et al. · PNAS
Targeting the cGAS-STING pathway reduces neuroinflammation and delays pathogenesis in a Huntington disease knock-in mouse model. → article
STING-OPTN signaling confers cytoprotection through TBK1-dependent mitophagy.
Jun 09, 2026 · Huang Ze-Bo, Lu Guang et al. · Cell Reports
STING activates selective mitophagy via TBK1-phosphorylated OPTN as a cytoprotective function independent of IFN signaling. → article
Characterization of a transmembrane-activating STING agonist using genetically humanized mice.
Jun 01, 2026 · Mizuno Nobuyo, DeFilippis Victor R et al. · Cell Reports
A transmembrane STING agonist activates innate immunity in genetically humanized mice, revealing human-specific STING pharmacology in vivo. → article
Proteomic prediction of APOL1 -associated kidney disease
Jun 12, 2026 · Kyle Vogan · Nature Genetics
Serum proteomic profiling identifies biomarkers predicting kidney disease risk and progression in individuals with APOL1 high-risk variants. → article

PAWR promotes antiviral innate immunity by transcriptionally upregulating RIG-I expression through a PIM2 kinase-XBP1s transcription factor axis. → article

Jun 11, 2026 · Fahmy Ahmed M, Desjardins Michel et al. · Cell Reports
STING dampens the unfolded protein response during inflammation to promote MHC-I-mediated presentation of mitochondrial self-antigens. → article
